name: Antonio Hart
abstract: Antonio Hart (born September 30, 1968) is an American jazz alto saxophonist. He attended the Baltimore School for the Arts, studied with Andy McGhee at Berklee College of Music, and has a master's degree from Queens College, City University of New York.

concerts.label: New England Symphonic Ensemble
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works.t: Every Day I Have The Blues

about_quotes.speaker: Urbie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And we heard Antonio Hart. [INTERVIEWER] Oh Antonio sounds great. [INTERVIEWEE] Well there’s some really terrific young players around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Urbie Green interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: And it’s been wonderful working with him because he’s a very serious — whatever he plays he’s very serious about it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes
about_quotes.evidence: Antonio Hart. These are the youngsters that are, that are carrying on authentic jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Schenectady, New York, March 28, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I actually had a chance to do my first record maybe six years ago when this quote unquote young lions craze started to happen with Roy Hargrove and Antonio Hart and all of us came on the scene together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
